content: Project: @cfxdevkit/framework (v2.0.0) — Conflux DevKit monorepo. Private npm workspace (pnpm@10.33.2, node>=24.15.0). Physical layout: repos/cfx-* (slices) + projects/* (apps) + docs/* + infrastructure/* + scripts/*. Uses moonrepo as task runner (not Turborepo/Nx/Bazel).

Architectural tiers (MUST NOT be violated):
- Tier -1 cross-cutting: repos/cfx-meta, repos/cfx-config (config/meta only, no runtime deps on higher tiers)
- Tier 0 framework: repos/cfx-core, repos/cfx-keys, repos/cfx-ui, repos/cfx-solidity (tree-shakeable, Node 20+ / modern browsers, no deps on any other tier)
- Tier 1 platform: repos/cfx-tools (mcp, scaffold, cli, editor tooling — depends on framework only, never runtime dep on deployed apps)
- Tier 2 domains: repos/cfx-domain (game-engine, automation — imports framework only)
- Tier 3 projects: projects/* (cas, chainbrawler, conflux-phaser, electro, examples — free to use any tier)
Conceptual tier names (framework/, platform/, domains/) are labels only — actual paths are repos/cfx-*/packages/.

OpenSpec workflow: schema=spec-driven. Four artifacts per change in order: proposal.md → design.md → specs/<cap>.md → tasks.md. Changes live in openspec/changes/<name>/. Archive to openspec/changes/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<name>/. Slash commands: /opsx:explore, /opsx:propose <name>, /opsx:apply [name], /opsx:archive [name]. CLI: openspec new change, openspec status --change, openspec instructions apply --change, openspec list --json.

Validation commands (run before committing or merging):
1. detect_changes() — check what changed (gitnexus)
2. pnpm check:hotspots — code complexity / cognitive complexity
3. pnpm lint — Biome linting
4. pnpm typecheck — TypeScript compilation
5. pnpm test — unit + integration tests
For OpenSpec changes: also run pnpm --filter <pkg> test for affected packages. Quality gates flag files >300 lines as violations.

Pi harness configuration (.pi/settings.json):
- Runtime: cdk agent, control-plane: cdk
- Provider source: .pi/providers.json (local endpoint http://localhost:28787/v1/)
- Default mode: deterministic
- Installed packages: npm:@davecodes/pi-dcp (compaction), npm:pi-gitnexus (code intelligence), ./repos/cfx-tools/infra/pi-extensions
- Skills loaded: .pi/skills, ./repos/cfx-tools/infra/pi-extensions/skills
- Project skills: .pi/skills/ (framework-check, gitnexus, openspec-*, librarian, repo-actions)

GitNexus index: framework type, 9475 symbols, 20380 relationships, 300 execution flows. Refresh: node .gitnexus/run.cjs analyze from repo root. Skills: gitnexus-exploring (how X works), gitnexus-impact-analysis (blast radius), gitnexus-debugging (why failing), gitnexus-refactoring (rename/extract/split).

Pi system prompt: project has AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md with GitNexus instructions. Context files concatenate from: ~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md, parent dirs, cwd. Custom system prompt via .pi/SYSTEM.md or ~/.pi/agent/SYSTEM.md. Append via APPEND_SYSTEM.md.

Pi compaction: auto-enabled, triggers on context overflow or approaching limit. Manual: /compact or /compact <custom instructions>. Long sessions: save change name, artifact paths, task state, validation outcomes, stale dist state, blocked tasks. Re-enter after compaction: openspec status --change <name> --json, openspec instructions apply --change <name> --json, cat tasks.md.

Project trust: Pi asks before trusting project folders with project-local settings/.agents/skills. Trust saved in ~/.pi/agent/trust.json. /trust command saves decision. Non-interactive modes use defaultProjectTrust (ask/always/never). Pass --approve/--no-approve to override for one run.

package.json scripts cleaned: 101 → 45. Removed duplicates: agent/llm (→ agent-exploratory), llm:commit/repo:commit, llm:wiki/docs:wiki, llm:review/repo:review, llm/agent, llm:ask (broken), llm:config→agent:config. Fixed broken: showcase → bash scripts/showcase-dev.sh (was moon run showcase), showcase:stop → bash scripts/stop-showcase.sh (was moon run showcase-stop), agent → moon run tooling-cli:agent-exploratory (was moon run tooling-cli:agent-chat which didn't exist). All 45 commands validated: no duplicates, all moon targets confirmed in moon.yml files, shell scripts tested end-to-end.
Sessions terminating at exactly 73 messages appeared twice, indicating a potential natural task completion depth or context threshold worth tracking.
